title: QUIC client-side replacement peer Initial reset architecture
purpose: Describe how the managed client/runtime path recognizes a replacement peer Initial that proves a new server attempt before transcript completion, resets the in-progress peer handshake attempt, and rederives later Handshake state without widening into general multi-attempt orchestration or Retry ownership.
risks: If the runtime resets on any peer Initial source-connection-ID change, same-attempt retransmissions can discard valid handshake progress and create new interop failures.

If the reset leaves the Initial ingress CRYPTO buffer, transcript offsets, or derived Handshake material partially intact, replacement-attempt packets can be mixed with the earlier attempt and corrupt the managed TLS state.
If the slice expands into general multi-attempt handshake orchestration or server-role behavior, it will overclaim support beyond the narrow client-side boundary proven here.
design_summary: While the client runtime is still establishing and before the peer transcript is complete, it records two pieces of identity for the first accepted peer Initial attempt: the peer Initial source connection ID and the first offset-zero Initial CRYPTO prefix. A later peer Initial is treated as a replacement attempt only when both of those markers change. That combination indicates a different server attempt rather than a same-attempt retransmission. Before the replacement Initial is applied, the runtime resets the current peer-handshake attempt state by clearing the Initial ingress CRYPTO buffer, transcript ingress offsets, and derived Handshake/open material that belonged to the earlier attempt. The replacement Initial is then processed through the normal Initial path so later Handshake and 1-RTT state are rederived from the replacement attempt. If only the source connection ID changes while the offset-zero Initial CRYPTO prefix remains the same, the runtime keeps the current attempt. Retry-owned one-replay bootstrap behavior remains separately traced under REQ-QUIC-CRT-0122.
alternatives_considered: Reset on any peer Initial source-connection-ID change. Rejected because ordinary retransmissions can vary source connection ID while still belonging to the same peer attempt.

Never reset once the first peer Initial is accepted. Rejected because the multiconnect replacement-server capture demonstrated that later Handshake packets can belong to a distinct peer attempt and require a clean rederive path.
Build a general multi-attempt handshake coordinator. Rejected because the bounded need here is only the client-side pre-transcript reset boundary for replacement Initial packets.
edge_cases_and_constraints: Only while the runtime is still establishing and before `PeerHandshakeTranscriptCompleted` is true.
The replacement discriminator uses both the peer Initial source connection ID and the first offset-zero Initial CRYPTO prefix.
The slice must preserve the existing Retry-owned one-replay bootstrap behavior from REQ-QUIC-CRT-0122.
The slice does not widen public API, transfer support, retry support claims, or general token lifecycle behavior.
